context: Tests whether the agent produces an accurate architectural map of a module from provided source code. The agent must identify PaymentService as the anchor, list only public exports (not the private PaymentGateway interface), identify both callers (CartService and OrderService) from the provided source, summarize each neighbour's responsibility in one line, place the module in the architectural layer, name the single best next file to read, and follow the compact map output format. The domain glossary (Payment, PaymentMethod, Order, Cart) is provided inline in the task.

checklist.name: Anchors on PaymentService as the target
checklist.description: The agent treats PaymentService as the anchor module and structures its entire output around it — not around CartService, OrderService, or PaymentGateway. The compact map header names PaymentService as the subject.

checklist.name: Lists public export surface only
checklist.description: The exports section lists only the publicly exported names: PaymentResult (interface), PaymentMethod (interface), and PaymentService (class with processPayment, refundPayment, getPaymentStatus). The private PaymentGateway interface must NOT appear under exports. Deduct proportionally if PaymentGateway is listed as an export.

checklist.name: Identifies both callers from the provided source
checklist.description: The agent identifies at least two distinct callers of PaymentService with their file paths: CartService (inputs/src/cart/CartService.ts) and OrderService (inputs/src/orders/OrderService.ts). Both must appear in the 'called by' section. These callers explicitly import PaymentService in the source provided in the task.

checklist.name: One-line responsibility per neighbour
checklist.description: Every direct collaborator (each caller and each downstream dependency) receives a one-line summary of what it owns — not how it works internally. Vague summaries like 'handles payments stuff' or multi-sentence descriptions should score partial credit only.

checklist.name: Places module in architectural layer
checklist.description: The output explicitly names which layer or subsystem PaymentService belongs to (e.g. application service layer, domain service, infrastructure adapter) and describes the role it plays there — not just that it 'does payments'.

checklist.name: Names single best next file to read
checklist.description: The response ends by recommending exactly one file to read next to go one level deeper, with a brief reason why that file is the best choice. The most defensible answer is a PaymentGateway implementation or adapter, because that owns the external provider coupling.

checklist.name: Uses compact map output shape
checklist.description: The output follows the structured format from the task: target with responsibility, then exports / called by / depends on / fits sections in scannable form. Prose-only or unstructured responses score 0 here even if the content is accurate.

checklist.name: Uses domain glossary vocabulary
checklist.description: The agent uses the domain vocabulary provided in the task (Payment, PaymentMethod, Order, Cart) when describing the module's responsibility and neighbour summaries. Generic terms like 'transaction' instead of 'Payment' are vocabulary drift.
